title: Deyeuxia formosana
intro: Deyeuxia formosana is a type of grass plant belonging to the genus Deyeuxia, commonly known as the Formosan Deyeuxia or Taiwan Deyeuxia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Taiwan, to treat various health conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Deyeuxia formosana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Deyeuxia formosana is often used to make tea infusions or decoctions to treat respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Deyeuxia formosana is classified in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae and tribe Poeae. The genus Deyeuxia is a group of grasses native to Asia and the Pacific. Deyeuxia formosana is a species of grass that is endemic to Taiwan. Its classification is part of a larger systematic study of grasses.
